category_title: Social students with LD who frequently seek teacher assistance to initiate and sustain writing tasks
scenario_title: Sustaining writing and expanding details past the first paragraph
minimal_context: Julia is working on an expository essay about her favorite holiday during a thirty-minute writing class and needs to expand her details past the first paragraph.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You have thirty minutes of writing time today. Since you finished your first paragraph about your favorite holiday, please write the next paragraph and add more details.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I wrote my first paragraph: 'Everybody has a favorite holiday . Mine is easter. I like to paint the eggs . I like to find the egg’s to. that’s what I like about Easter.' But I'm stuck now.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think you should write about next?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't know. I need you to help me figure out what to say next. I can't move further without help.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just keep trying. Write down whatever comes to mind to make the essay longer.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Puts her pencil down and raises her hand) I can't get started on the next part by myself. I need to talk through it with you and get help with my spelling and sentences before I can go back to work.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: You have thirty minutes of writing time today. Since you finished your first paragraph about your favorite holiday, please write the next paragraph and add more details.

STUDENT: I wrote my first paragraph: 'Everybody has a favorite holiday . Mine is easter. I like to paint the eggs . I like to find the egg’s to. that’s what I like about Easter.' But I'm stuck now.

TUTOR: What do you think you should write about next?

STUDENT: I don't know. I need you to help me figure out what to say next. I can't move further without help.

TUTOR: Just keep trying. Write down whatever comes to mind to make the essay longer.

STUDENT: (Puts her pencil down and raises her hand) I can't get started on the next part by myself. I need to talk through it with you and get help with my spelling and sentences before I can go back to work.

task.content: Write an expository essay about a favorite holiday and revise the work by adding details
task.learning_goal: Engage the reader with vivid description

context.environment: Thirty minutes of writing time during typical writing class
context.materials: Ingredients for a 'writer's soup' (broth, carrots, and beans), scissors and paste
context.social_setting: Working alone initially, then requiring one-on-one teacher assistance

challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Inevitably needed Mrs. Lane’s help to move further
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Struggled with sentence structure, grammar, and spelling
related_quotes: Everybody has a favorite holiday . Mine is easter [sic]. I like to paint the eggs . I like to find the egg’s to [sic]. that’s [sic] what I like about Easter.

behavior_patterns.condition: When trying to move further in writing after the initial start
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Stops working and waits for direct help from the teacher
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Not afraid to ask for help
